zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Richard Pierce ,Percy於1500年出生在英國越可爾托普可利福時，他的父親Sir Peter Percy53歲，母親Lady Annah "Anne" James, Baroness Hungerford and Percy46歲。他於1531年與Eleanor Acton在英國英格蘭越可爾結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。他去世於1553年在英國英格蘭越可爾享年53歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Richard Pierce ,Percy于1500年出生在英国越可爾托普可利福时，他的父亲Sir Peter Percy53岁，母亲Lady Annah "Anne" James, Baroness Hungerford and Percy46岁。他于1531年与Eleanor Acton在英国英格兰越可爾结婚。他们至少是2个儿子的父母。他去世于1553年在英国英格兰越可爾享年53岁。

nameMeanings.name: Pierce
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Welsh, and Irish: from the Anglo-Norman French and Middle English personal name Piers, Peres, Peris, an Old French nominative of Pierre or Per, the French form of Peter . Pierce is especially frequent in northwestern Wales and Lancashire, while Pearce is the more common spelling in the rest of England. In Ireland Pierce and Pearse represent a shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Piarais ‘son of Piaras’, a Gaelicized form of Piers.
Americanized form of some similar-sounding Jewish surname.
History: Franklin Pierce (1804–69), 14th president of the US, was born in Hillsborough, NH, on the New England frontier. His English ancestor Thomas Pierce emigrated to Charlestown, MA, in 1633/34.

variants.name: Pyers
meanings: English (North Yorkshire) and Scottish (Ayrshire): variant of   Pierce  . This surname is rare in Britain.

variants.name: Peirce
meanings: English: variant of   Pierce  .
History: The name Peirce first appears in colonial American records in 1623with William Peirce, an English shipmaster who compiled the firstalmanac in English America.

variants.name: Piers
meanings: English and Irish (of Norman origin): variant of   Pierce  .

variants.name: Perrell
meanings: English (Warwickshire):
from a short form of the Middle English personal name Peres, Peris (from Old French Pierre; see Pierce ) + the diminutive suffix -el.
habitational name from a lost or unidentified place.

variants.name: Pearce
meanings: Welsh, English, and Irish: variant of   Pierce  .

variants.name: Pearse
meanings: English: variant of   Pierce  .

variants.name: Peter
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Kurt, Otto, Ernst, Fritz, Heinz, Helmut, Horst, Kaspar, Klaus, Siegfried, Wolfgang.

English, Scottish, German, Dutch, French (Alsace and Lorraine), Czech (Moravian), Slovak, Croatian, and Slovenian; Hungarian (Péter): from the personal name Peter (Greek Petros, from petra ‘rock’). The personal name was popular throughout Christian Europe in the Middle Ages, having been bestowed by Christ as a byname on the apostle Simon bar Jonah, the brother of Andrew. The name was chosen by Christ for its symbolic significance (John 1:42, Matt. 16:18); Saint Peter is regarded as the founding head of the Christian Church in view of Christ's saying, ‘Thou art Peter and upon this rock I will build my Church’. In Christian Germany in the early Middle Ages this was the most frequent personal name of non-ancient Germanic origin until the 14th century. In North America, this surname has also absorbed cognates from other languages, for example Czech Petr , Polish Piotr and Pietr, Albanian Pjetri (from the personal name Pjetër, definite form Pjetri), and also their derivatives (see examples at Peterson ). It has also been adopted as a surname by Ashkenazic Jews.

variants.name: Pears
meanings: English: variant of   Pierce  .

variants.name: Peairs
meanings: English (Durham): variant of   Pierce  .

variants.name: Parkin
meanings: English (mainly Yorkshire): from the Middle English personal name Perkin, Parkin, a pet form of Peres ‘Peter’ (see Pierce ) with the diminutive suffix -kin. (The change from -er- to -ar- was a characteristic phonetic development in Middle English.)

nameMeanings.name: ,Percy
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin):

nickname from Old French percehaie ‘pierce hedge’ (Old French percer ‘to pierce, penetrate’ + haie ‘hedge, fence’), perhaps with the sense of someone breaking into an enclosure. Percehaie is the name of one of the sons of the foxes Renart and Hermeline in the medieval French epic Roman de Renart, whose earliest known version is from the 1170s. The surname is older than that, but it may originate in a nickname for the fox as ‘enclosure piercer’, perhaps amounting to ‘chicken thief’.

habitational name from any of several places called Percy in Calvados, Eure, and Manche; William de Perci, the Domesday tenant-in-chief and under-tenant of Hugh, Earl of Chester, came from either Percy-en-Auge (Eure) or Percy (Manche).

variants.name: Piercey
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Percy  .

meanings: English: variant of   Percy  .

meanings: English: variant of   Percy  .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Richard Pierce ,Percy was born in 1500, in Topcliffe, Yorkshire, England, his father, Sir Peter Percy, was 53 and his mother, Lady Annah "Anne" James, Baroness Hungerford and Percy, was 46. He married Eleanor Acton in 1531, in Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. He died in 1553, in Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 53.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Richard Pierce ,Percy est né en 1500, à Topcliffe, Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, Sir Peter Percy, avait 53 ans et sa mère, Lady Annah "Anne" James, Baroness Hungerford and Percy, avait 46 ans. Il a épousé Eleanor Acton en 1531, à Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Il est décédé en 1553, à Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 53 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Sir Richard Pierce ,Percy 1500 in Topcliffe, Yorkshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Sir Peter Percy 53 und seine Mutter Lady Annah "Anne" James, Baroness Hungerford and Percy 46. Er hat 1531 in Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom Eleanor Acton geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne. Er starb 1553 im Alter von 53 Jahren in Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Richard Pierce ,Percy è nato il nel 1500 nel Topcliffe, Yorkshire, England, il suo padre, Sir Peter Percy, aveva 53 anni e il suo madre, Lady Annah "Anne" James, Baroness Hungerford and Percy, aveva 46 anni. Lui sposato Eleanor Acton nel 1531, nel Yorkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli. Morì nel 1553, nel Yorkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 53 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Richard Pierce ,Percyが1500年にイギリスヨークシュアイアトプクリフで生まれた時，父Sir Peter Percyは53歳，母Lady Annah "Anne" James, Baroness Hungerford and Percyは46歳であった。1531年にイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアでEleanor Actonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子の親であった。彼は1553年にイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアで53歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Richard Pierce ,Percy(이)가 1500년 영국 요르크실르 토pc리프에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Sir Peter Percy(은)는 53세였고, 그의 어머니 Lady Annah "Anne" James, Baroness Hungerford and Percy(은)는 46세였다. 그는 1531년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르에서 Eleanor Acton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1553년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르에서 53세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sir Richard Pierce ,Percy nasceu em 1500, em Topcliffe, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir Peter Percy, tinha 53 anos e sua mãe, Lady Annah "Anne" James, Baroness Hungerford and Percy, tinha 46 anos. Ele casou-se com Eleanor Acton em 1531, em Yorkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos. Ele faleceu em 1553, em Yorkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 53 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Richard Pierce ,Percy родился в 1500 г. в местности топклифф, еркшир, Англия, его отцу по имени Sir Peter Percy было 53 года, а матери, которую звали Lady Annah "Anne" James, Baroness Hungerford and Percy, 46. Он женился на женщине по имени Eleanor Acton в 1531 г. в местности еркшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Он умер в 1553 г. в местности еркшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 53 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Sir Richard Pierce ,Percy nació en 1500, en Topcliffe, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir Peter Percy, tenía 53 años y su mamá, Lady Annah "Anne" James, Baroness Hungerford and Percy, tenía 46 años. Se casó con Eleanor Acton en 1531, en Yorkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos. Murió en 1553, en Yorkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 53 años.
